doc_title: Draft METHOD 327 - Fugitive and Area Source Measurement of Selected Volatile Organic Hazardous Air Pollutants Using Specially Prepared Canisters

steps.text: Collect a whole air sample through a particulate filter with a flow control device into an evacuated, specially prepared canister for the specified length of time.
Measure the canister pressure in the field.
Transport the canister to the laboratory for analysis.
Verify the sample collection information upon receipt at the laboratory.
Measure the canister pressure at the laboratory.
Store the canister at ambient laboratory temperature until analysis.
Direct a known volume of the sample from the canister into a preconcentrator.

preconditions.text: Ensure the canister is evacuated, certified, and clean.

constraints.text: Do not use butyl rubber or perfluoroalkoxy (PFA) materials.
Do not use regulators that contain PFA materials and avoid Teflon products where possible.
Do not use PTFE thread sealants or Buna-N rubber components on any wetted surface.
Use only NIST-certified or NIST-traceable calibration standards, standard reference materials, and reagents.
